title: A Fireworks Truck Exploded on Tennessee's I-75. No One Was Hurt.
script: Fireworks on the highway! On June 7, a pickup truck hauling a trailer full of fireworks caught fire on Interstate 75 North in Hamilton County, Tennessee, near the Ooltewah exit. Within minutes, the entire load detonated. Fireworks shot hundreds of feet into the air directly over the highway as cars backed up for miles. Both northbound and southbound lanes of I-75 shut down for hours. Miraculously, no one was injured. The Tennessee Highway Patrol cited the driver for failing to have a required hazardous materials endorsement, missing hazmat placards, and lacking proper documentation. Federal rules require special licensing to haul that kind of cargo. Video captured by drivers went viral, showing a full Fourth-of-July-style show over the freeway at rush hour. Could Tennessee's hazmat enforcement prevent the next one?
